rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL LIND ET AL VS. AERON NOWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2021 line 1. PETITIONERS MICHAEL LIND and KARA LIND's Amended Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOSE GOLAW ET AL VS. HENRY ROSENTHAL AND CAROLA ANDERSON, INDIVIDUALLY ET AL

PLAINTIFFS JOSE GOLAW and BRITTANY FROMM's Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint In Each Action is GRANTED in part. Motion for leave to amend in action 583200 is granted. No opposition filed. To the extent motion purports to seek a leave to amend inaction 583202 the motion is denied as it is not properly calendared in that action. rulings.Case Title: JOANNA PFEISTER VS. ACTION PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joanna Pfeister'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Rendering Judgment By Default Against Defendant Pursuant To Ccp 2023.030(D)(4) For Disobeying The Court Order To Provide Discovery Under Ccp 2023.010(G)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2021 line 8. Plaintiff Joanna Pfeister's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Rendering Judgment By Default Against Defendant Pursuant To Ccp 2023.030(D)(4) For Disobeying The Court Order To Provide Discovery Under Ccp 2023.010(G) is GRANTED. The order will be stayed and vacated if Defendants (1) produce unredacted copies (as previously order by judge Schulman; that decision will not be re-visited and reconsidered by this Court) of all communications between the HOA and its attorneys between December 1, 2018 and July 2, 2019, including invoices for legal services, no later than May 14, 2021; and (2) turn over the mailing addresses of all members no later than May 14, 2021, unless they have specifically opted out. Any further proceedings by Defendants in this action are stayed until May 21, 2021. Monetary sanctions awarded in the amount of costs (to be presented at the hearing) and to be paid no later than May 14, 2021. Each defendant is also sanctioned in the amount of $1,000 payable to the Court to be paid no later than May 14, 2021 The stay of this order discussed above does not apply to monetary sanctions. No later than May 21, 2021 Defendants to file declarations of compliance and to appear ex-parte with a proposed order vacating the order as outlined above. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
